test_description: Compare the same summary note inside continuous book reading versus opening the excerpt cold in isolation.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether book-context sufficiency is being falsely projected onto excerpt-library conditions.
why_this_test_matters: This is the core tension in the owner’s answer.

test_description: Compare a purely orientational reference-back note to a concept-building one that the author immediately expands from.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Failure to distinguish light reminders from dependency-heavy recalls.
why_this_test_matters: SC1 hinges on this exact distinction.

test_description: Compare excerpt-to-excerpt links alone versus an openable full passage or chapter reference layer.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether excerpt chains become chaotic where passage-level restoration is actually needed.
why_this_test_matters: The owner explicitly warns that pure excerpt chains may become the system’s demise in such cases.

test_description: Open the unit with zero pre-context and measure whether the owner must manually search for the earlier material.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Residual manhunt burden despite supposed self-containment.
why_this_test_matters: The owner’s standard is that the system should provide the path itself.

test_description: Simulate a reader who has not read the previous chapter and does not remember the earlier discussion.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: How much the current unit depends on broken author-flow assumptions.
why_this_test_matters: This is the actual excerpt-library user model the owner wants respected.

test_description: Expand the unit with guidance layers until source centrality starts to blur, then inspect where discipline fails.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether teaching-unit expansion remains bounded or becomes chaotic.
why_this_test_matters: The owner explicitly wants ambition without protocol collapse.

test_description: Replace source-preserving context restoration with a generated note only.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether synthetic notes silently displace the source instead of supporting it.
why_this_test_matters: The case pushes strongly toward source-centered support, not note-only substitution.

test_description: Hide the original full passage while leaving only the short summary note visible.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: How much of the lost context is actually restored by the note alone.
why_this_test_matters: The owner explicitly values full passage visibility as the missing link.

test_description: Label every reference-back note as 'orientation only' and inspect which cases then become conceptually unsafe.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Misclassification of concept-building reference-backs as harmless reminders.
why_this_test_matters: This misclassification is one of the core structural risks exposed by SC1.
